# omada_ip_group (Resource)

Manages a reusable IP group on the Omada controller (Settings → Profiles → Groups), usable as a source/destination in firewall ACLs.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "omada_ip_group" "lab_networks" {
  name = "lab-networks"
  ip_list = [
    { ip = "10.10.10.0", mask = 24, description = "clients" },
    { ip = "10.10.20.0", mask = 24, description = "services" },
  ]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `ip_list` (Attributes List) IPv4 CIDR entries in the group. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--ip_list))
- `name` (String) Group name.

### Optional

- `site` (String) Site name. Defaults to the primary site. Changing this forces replacement.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Controller-assigned group ID.
- `site_id` (String) Resolved site ID.

<a id="nestedatt--ip_list"></a>
### Nested Schema for `ip_list`

Required:

- `ip` (String) Network address, e.g. `10.10.10.0`.
- `mask` (Number) Prefix length (1-32).

Optional:

- `description` (String) Optional per-entry description.
